message:
fcp-conformance: extend policy matrix to all 5 canonical zones

Audit of the zone × capability conformance matrix found three coverage
gaps. Each gap is a pinned contract that matrix was supposed to
protect but didn't.

[MEDIUM] public zone (untrusted-edge) absent from the matrix
  FCP declares five canonical zones (owner, private, work, community,
  public). The matrix only exercised three. The public zone is the
  most important conformance surface — it's the untrusted-edge
  enforcement boundary — and was completely unverified.
  Fix: add the public zone with a capability_allow policy that
  exercises the `ZonePolicyCapabilityNotAllowed` deny path.

[LOW] owner zone (highest-trust) absent from the matrix
  No cell asserted that the wide-open "admin" zone allows everything.
  Fix: add owner with empty allow/deny/ceiling — the canonical
  permissive configuration.

[LOW] `ZonePolicyCapabilityNotAllowed` reason code unreachable
  The matrix only tripped `CapabilityInsufficient` (ceiling path) and
  `ZonePolicyCapabilityDenied` (blocklist path). The third distinct
  capability-rejection reason — `ZonePolicyCapabilityNotAllowed`,
  fired when `capability_allow` is non-empty and the cap isn't in it
  — was never reached. A regression that collapsed these three codes
  into one would have gone undetected.
  Fix: pin all three with an explicit distinctness test.

[LOW] `capability_deny` vs `capability_ceiling` precedence not pinned
  When both gates would reject the same cap, the code denies with
  `ZonePolicyCapabilityDenied` (blocklist wins). That order is
  operator-observable in decision receipts — swapping it would mask
  explicit blocklist intent behind a generic ceiling reason.
  Fix: add a precedence test that sets both gates on the same cap
  and asserts blocklist wins.

Matrix is now 5 zones × 3 capabilities = 15 cells, plus two new
pinning tests (distinct reasons, deny-beats-ceiling precedence).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
